aliases.note: ABA2 (ABA DEFICIENT 2) encodes a short-chain dehydrogenase/reductase in the ABA biosynthetic pathway; aba2 loss-of-function reduces bulk ABA, so perturbation maps to the ABA hormone node (10.1105/tpc.006007).

CCD7 (MAX3) is a carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase that produces 9-cis-beta-apo-10'-carotenal upstream of CARLACTONE; max3 KO eliminates strigolactone, so the test maps to the Strigolactone hormone node (10.1101/gad.1164403).
CCD8 (MAX4) converts the CCD7 product into CARLACTONE, the direct SL precursor; ccd8 KO abolishes strigolactone biosynthesis, so the perturbation maps to the Strigolactone node (10.1038/nature07271).

D27 is the beta-carotene isomerase that feeds CCD7/CCD8 to produce CARLACTONE; d27 loss reduces CARLACTONE (and downstream strigolactone), so the test maps to the CARLACTONE precursor node present in the KB (10.1073/pnas.1322673111).

FLOWERING LOCUS T is a mobile florigen; its branching role acts through HB21/HB40/HB53 + BRC1 rather than a direct KB node, and no KB entry (e.g., 'production of long-d') is unambiguously FT. Mark not_in_network.
HB21, HB40 and HB53 are HD-Zip I paralogs acting redundantly downstream of BRC1 to arrest bud outgrowth via NCED3/ABA; HB53 is the family member present in the KB (10.1073/pnas.1704379114).
NCED3 (9-cis-epoxycarotenoid dioxygenase 3) catalyses the rate-limiting step of ABA biosynthesis; nced3 KO reduces ABA in buds, so the perturbation maps to the ABA hormone node (10.1105/tpc.010289).

TIE1 (TCP INTERACTOR CONTAINING EAR MOTIF 1) recruits TPL/TPR co-repressors to class-II TCPs, dampening BRC1 activity; perturbation of TIE1 acts functionally on BRC1, the closest KB node (10.1105/tpc.113.117473).
